content.text: I found existing compiler tests for arrays in mappings and the panic location; I’ll inspect those next to understand the intended validation path.
I found an existing test for arrays as mapping values, so I’m narrowing this to mapping key type validation and the SSA panic path.

17	use crate::Type;
18	
19	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
20	use std::fmt;
21	
22	/// A mapping type of a key and value type.
23	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
24	pub struct MappingType {
25	    pub key: Box<Type>,
26	    pub value: Box<Type>,
27	}
28	
29	impl fmt::Display for MappingType {
30	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter) -> fmt::Result {
31	        write!(f, "({} => {})", self.key, self.value)
32	    }
33	}
34

content.text:
Implemented a fix for the mapping-array-key panic.

What changed:
- Updated `crates/passes/src/type_checking/program.rs`
- Mapping definitions now reject non-empty array key types during type checking:
  - `mapping data: [address; 2] => u128;` now produces a normal compiler error instead of reaching later passes and potentially panicking.
- Preserved existing behavior for zero-sized array keys so the existing `empty_type_mappings_fail` expectation remains unchanged.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary regression test for:
  - `mapping data: [address; 2] => u128;`
- Confirmed it reports:
  - `A mapping's key cannot be a non-empty array`
- Reran the temporary regression test successfully.
- Deleted the temporary test and expectation files afterward as requested.
- Reran the existing mappings compiler suite:
  - `TEST_FILTER=compiler/mappings cargo test -p leo-compiler test_compiler -- --nocapture`
  - Passed: 13 tests.

Notes:
- I did not commit any changes.
- Final intentional working-tree change is only:
  - `crates/passes/src/type_checking/program.rs`
- There are unrelated pre-existing untracked items still present:
  - `reports/`
  - `tests/expectations/compiler/dynamic_dispatch/static_external_future.out`

result:
Implemented a fix for the mapping-array-key panic.

What changed:
- Updated `crates/passes/src/type_checking/program.rs`
- Mapping definitions now reject non-empty array key types during type checking:
  - `mapping data: [address; 2] => u128;` now produces a normal compiler error instead of reaching later passes and potentially panicking.
- Preserved existing behavior for zero-sized array keys so the existing `empty_type_mappings_fail` expectation remains unchanged.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary regression test for:
  - `mapping data: [address; 2] => u128;`
- Confirmed it reports:
  - `A mapping's key cannot be a non-empty array`
- Reran the temporary regression test successfully.
- Deleted the temporary test and expectation files afterward as requested.
- Reran the existing mappings compiler suite:
  - `TEST_FILTER=compiler/mappings cargo test -p leo-compiler test_compiler -- --nocapture`
  - Passed: 13 tests.

Notes:
- I did not commit any changes.
- Final intentional working-tree change is only:
  - `crates/passes/src/type_checking/program.rs`
- There are unrelated pre-existing untracked items still present:
  - `reports/`
  - `tests/expectations/compiler/dynamic_dispatch/static_external_future.out`
